title: aronia arbutifolia var. depressa
intro: Aronia arbutifolia var. depressa, also known as the black chokeberry, is a deciduous shrub native to the southeastern United States. The plant's dark purple berries, which are rich in anthocyanins and other antioxidants, have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. Research suggests that Aronia arbutifolia var. depressa may have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and cardiovascular benefits, including reducing the risk of heart disease and cancer. Aronia arbutifolia var. depressa is often prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is also used in jams and jellies due to its high antioxidant content.
